body_txt: Some villains oppose not only one individual, but their entire family. This antagonism is passed from generation to generation, especially if said villain is immortal and the family is of a Heroic Lineage , with only its descendants having the power needed to defeat the villain. While such a villain may accumulate power solely for themselves , a lineage of heroes represents a willingness to give up one's metaphorical time in the spotlight and make way for a new generation, the concept of which is entirely puzzling to the villain. A hero who belongs to a lineage may be unafraid to die as they know they will be succeeded, while the immortal villain will be a Dirty Coward who greatly fears the prospect of death . Often times children will take up their parents' work when they are retired or dead and defeat the Familial Foe in their name. The Family Foe may seek a Family Extermination if they present enough of a threat. May overlap with You Killed My Father and Hero Killer . Compare Feuding Families and Sins of Our Fathers . Not to be confused with Archnemesis Dad and Evil Matriarch , despite the names sounding similar. The trope also occasionally applies to heroic characters who fight multiple generations of a villainous family.
